# React Dynamic Forms — Modernization & AI-Native Rebirth

**Status:** Draft v1
**Date:** 2026-04-19
**Author:** Roger Oliveira
**Target audience:** contributors, future maintainers, AI coding agents

---

## 1. Vision

Turn `react-dynamic-forms` into the **go-to Zod-first dynamic form library for React in 2026**, with AI-powered schema generation as its viral launch feature.

**Tagline:** *Dynamic React forms, powered by Zod. Validated by types. Generated by AI.*

**Positioning:** sits in the JSON-Schema / schema-driven form-renderer space (competing with `@rjsf/core`, `@jsonforms/react`, `@autoform/react`), but differentiates by:

1. **Zod-first developer experience** — write a Zod schema, get a fully validated form in one line.
2. **Hybrid schema input** — also accepts JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 for persistence/backend interop.
3. **shadcn/ui under the hood** — beautiful out of the box, fully headless/themeable.
4. **AI-native (v2)** — prompt → schema → form in seconds, using the Vercel AI SDK.
5. **Tiny & headless core** — < 15 kb gzipped, tree-shakable, Radix slot pattern for component override.

**Success signals:**
- npm weekly downloads > 1 k within 3 months of v1 launch
- GitHub stars > 500 within 6 months
- Featured in at least one newsletter (React Newsletter, This Week in React, JavaScript Weekly)
- Organic adoption (3+ unrelated projects using it within 6 months)

## 2. Strategy: two-phase release

### Phase 1 — Foundation (v1.0.0)
Solid, modern, publishable. No AI yet. This is the "it just works" release.

### Phase 2 — AI launch (v2.0.0)
AI generator + conditional fields + multi-step wizard + killer docs site. **This is the marketed release** — blog post, Twitter thread, Product Hunt, r/reactjs, dev.to.

Phase 1 and Phase 2 ship as separate major versions so the base is battle-tested before the launch moment.

## 3. Repository restructure

From a single-folder CRA app to a **pnpm + Turborepo monorepo**:

```
react-dynamic-forms/
├── apps/
│   ├── docs/                 Next.js 15 + Fumadocs + live playground (Vercel)
│   └── demo/                 Vite playground app (legacy demos, migration guide)
├── packages/
│   ├── core/                 @rogeroliveira84/react-dynamic-forms (renderer + schema adapters)
│   ├── ui/                   shadcn registry components (copy via CLI)
│   └── ai/                   @rogeroliveira84/rdf-ai (v2 — prompt → schema)
├── .claude/                  CLAUDE.md + commands + subagents + settings
├── .github/workflows/        GitHub Actions: test, release (Changesets), deploy
├── docs/superpowers/specs/   Design docs (this file)
├── changeset/                Changesets config
├── turbo.json
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml
├── package.json
└── README.md                 Marketing-first (see §10)
```

**Why monorepo:** lib + docs site + registry need to evolve together, share types, and deploy in sync. Turborepo gives caching + parallel builds.

**Dropped from repo:** CRA (`react-scripts`), Enzyme, `serviceWorker.js`, old Dockerfile (Node 11), CircleCI config, `_config.yml` (Jekyll).

## 4. Public API

### 4.1 The `<DynamicForm>` component

```tsx
import { DynamicForm } from '@rogeroliveira84/react-dynamic-forms'
import { z } from 'zod'

const schema = z.object({
  email: z.string().email(),
  age: z.number().min(18).max(120),
  newsletter: z.boolean().default(false),
  country: z.enum(['BR', 'US', 'PT']),
})

export function MyForm() {
  return (
    <DynamicForm
      schema={schema}
      onSubmit={(data) => console.log(data)}
      submitLabel="Sign up"
    />
  )
}
```

### 4.2 Supported schema inputs

| Input type | Example |
|---|---|
| Zod schema | `z.object({ ... })` |
| JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 | `{ "type": "object", "properties": { ... } }` |
| Legacy v0.5 config | `{ fields: [{ id, label, type, ... }] }` (through migration adapter, deprecated warning) |

The component auto-detects the input type at runtime (Zod has the `_def` brand; JSON Schema has `$schema` or `type: 'object'`; legacy has `fields: [...]`).

### 4.3 UI customization

```tsx
<DynamicForm
  schema={schema}
  components={{
    TextField: MyCustomTextField,
    SubmitButton: MyCustomButton,
  }}
  className="my-form"
  layout="grid"          // 'stack' | 'grid' | 'inline'
  theme={myShadcnTheme}
/>
```

The component library is **headless by default**: every field component receives props and can be replaced via the `components` prop (Radix slot pattern).

### 4.4 Hooks for advanced control

```tsx
import { useDynamicForm } from '@rogeroliveira84/react-dynamic-forms'

const form = useDynamicForm({ schema })
// returns: { form, fields, register, handleSubmit, errors, reset, ... }
// thin wrapper around react-hook-form's useForm with schema metadata
```

For users who want to render their own markup while still getting schema-driven state and validation.

## 5. Internal architecture

### 5.1 Schema pipeline

```
User input (Zod | JSON Schema | Legacy config)
    │
    ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  Schema detector    │  runtime type guard
└──────────┬──────────┘
           ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  Adapter            │  Zod→IS, JSONSchema→IS, Legacy→IS
└──────────┬──────────┘
           ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│ InternalSchema (IS) │  canonical form: { fields: FieldSpec[] }
└──────────┬──────────┘
           ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│ react-hook-form     │  register + validate via @hookform/resolvers/zod
│ + field components  │
└─────────────────────┘
```

**InternalSchema** is the lingua franca. Defining it clearly lets any future schema format (OpenAPI, Prisma, TypeBox) plug in by writing a new adapter.

### 5.2 FieldSpec type (canonical)

```ts
type FieldSpec =
  | { kind: 'text'; name: string; label?: string; validation: ZodString; ...}
  | { kind: 'number'; name: string; label?: string; validation: ZodNumber; ...}
  | { kind: 'boolean'; ... }
  | { kind: 'date'; ... }
  | { kind: 'enum'; options: {value,label}[]; ... }
  | { kind: 'multi-enum'; ... }
  | { kind: 'array'; item: FieldSpec; ... }      // repeater
  | { kind: 'object'; fields: FieldSpec[]; ... } // nested
```

### 5.3 Package boundaries

- `packages/core` — pure logic (adapters, types, hook). Zero UI deps. Consumers import components from `packages/ui` or bring their own. **No `react-dom` coupling** beyond what `react-hook-form` needs.
- `packages/ui` — shadcn components pre-wired to `core`. Installable via `npx shadcn add rdf-text-field` against a hosted registry.
- `packages/ai` (v2) — prompt → schema generator. Depends on `core` types only.

Each package publishes its own ESM + CJS bundle via `tsup`, with `.d.ts`.

## 6. Field types (v1 scope)

Must-ship in v1:

- `text`, `email`, `password`, `url` (HTML5 input variants)
- `number` (with min/max/step)
- `textarea`
- `boolean` → checkbox or switch (auto-chosen by Zod metadata)
- `date`, `datetime-local`, `time`
- `enum` → select or radio group
- `multi-enum` → multi-select (combobox)
- `slider` (number with `.min().max()` + metadata)
- `object` (nested sub-form)
- `array` (repeater with add/remove)

**Deferred to v2:**
- `file` (upload with preview/progress)
- `combobox` with async options
- `richtext` (tiptap)
- `signature`
- `geo` / map picker

All fields render shadcn/ui components by default. All fields are WCAG AA accessible (keyboard nav, ARIA, visible focus).

## 7. Validation & errors

- Validation flows through `@hookform/resolvers/zod` → instant field-level feedback.
- Error messages pulled from Zod's `.refine({ message })` or `z.string().email({ message: "..." })`.
- i18n-ready: error messages accept a resolver function `(ctx) => string`; v1 ships with English; v2 adds i18n plugin.
- Field-level async validation supported via Zod's `.refine(async (v) => ..., { message })`.

## 8. Testing strategy

### Stack
- **Vitest** (not Jest — faster, ESM native, plays with Vite)
- **React Testing Library** (not Enzyme — Enzyme is dead)
- **Playwright** for end-to-end docs site + form flows
- **`@axe-core/playwright`** for accessibility assertions
- **Storybook 8** inside `apps/docs` for component-level visual testing (optional, post-v1)

### Coverage targets
- `packages/core` schema adapters: **≥ 90 %** branch coverage (edge cases are where dynamic forms break)
- Field components: happy path + error state + a11y snapshot
- One end-to-end test per field kind through the demo app
- CI fails below 85 % on `core`.

### What we DON'T test
- Third-party internals (react-hook-form, Zod — they have their own tests)
- Exact shadcn CSS class names (brittle)

## 9. CI/CD and publishing

### GitHub Actions workflows

- `ci.yml` — on PR: install, lint, typecheck, test, build. Matrix: Node 20 + 22. Cache pnpm + Turborepo.
- `release.yml` — on push to `main`: Changesets action opens "Version Packages" PR or publishes to npm when merged.
- `preview.yml` — docs site preview deploy to Vercel on PR.
- `production.yml` — docs site promotion to production on merge to `main`.

**Changesets** flow:
1. Contributor runs `pnpm changeset` → writes a markdown file describing the change.
2. Bot aggregates into "Version Packages" PR.
3. Merging the PR publishes to npm and creates a GitHub release with changelog.

### CircleCI → GitHub Actions migration
Delete `.circleci/config.yml`. Travis config (`.travis.yml`) also gone. Badges in README updated.

### npm publish
- Keep current scoped package name `@rogeroliveira84/react-dynamic-forms` for continuity (users exist, downloads history matters).
- Add secondary unscoped package `react-dynamic-forms` if available (check). Redirect docs to scoped version as canonical.

## 10. README (marketing-first)

The README is the landing page for ~80 % of visitors. Structure:

1. **Hero** — logo/wordmark + tagline + animated GIF of "schema → form" in 3 seconds.
2. **Badges** — npm version, bundle size (Bundlephobia), stars, license, downloads/month.
3. **Why RDF?** — 5–6 bullet points with emojis (the elevator pitch).
4. **Quick start** — `pnpm add` + 10-line code example.
5. **Live playground link** — "Try it without installing" (docs site).
6. **Feature comparison table** — RDF vs RJSF vs JSON Forms vs react-hook-form (honest, no trash-talk).
7. **Roadmap** — v1 shipped, v2 coming, v3 ideas.
8. **Sponsors / contributors** — grid of avatars.
9. **License + credits**.

Tone: *confident, friendly, visual, a bit cheeky*. Emojis used purposefully (1 per section heading, not decoration in body). Screenshots in real dark-mode shadcn forms.

A separate `README.pt-br.md` in Portuguese is a nice-to-have for early organic reach in the LATAM dev community.

## 11. `.claude/` folder contents

Purpose: make the project navigable by AI coding agents AND onboard human contributors.

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 Project context, conventions, commands, architecture
├── settings.json             Hooks (pre-commit: lint/typecheck/test), permissions
├── commands/
│   ├── release.md            /release — run changeset + publish flow
│   ├── new-field.md          /new-field — scaffold a new field kind end-to-end
│   ├── new-example.md        /new-example — add an example to the docs site
│   └── schema-adapter.md     /schema-adapter — scaffold a new schema adapter (Prisma, OpenAPI, etc)
└── agents/
    ├── form-field-builder.md    Subagent: builds a new field kind (spec → FieldSpec → UI component → tests → docs)
    └── docs-writer.md            Subagent: keeps docs site synced with lib changes
```

### `CLAUDE.md` outline
- **What is this project** — 3-sentence summary
- **Stack** — React 19, TS strict, Zod, RHF, shadcn, pnpm+Turborepo, Vitest, GitHub Actions
- **How to run** — `pnpm dev`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm docs`
- **Monorepo map** — which package does what
- **Coding conventions** — TS strict, no default exports, co-locate tests, prefer Zod .refine over runtime checks
- **Common tasks** — "add a new field kind", "add a schema adapter", "write a new example"
- **Release process** — Changesets flow
- **Forbidden** — don't add Enzyme, CRA, or default-export React components

### `settings.json` outline
- Hooks: `PostToolUse` on Edit/Write → run `pnpm lint --filter affected` + `pnpm typecheck --filter affected` on the changed package
- Permissions: allow `pnpm`, `git status/log/diff`, `gh pr`, `npm view`, deny broad `rm -rf` outside workspace

## 12. Migration strategy from v0.5

Existing users have code like:
```jsx
import DynamicForm from '@rogeroliveira84/react-dynamic-forms'
<DynamicForm config={configJson} onSubmit={fn} />
```

v1 **keeps** this working for 1 major version via a legacy adapter:
- `config={...}` prop continues to work, emits a `console.warn` with a migration link.
- v2 drops the legacy adapter and `config` prop.

`docs/migrate-from-v0.md` covers:
1. "Replace `config` with `schema` (Zod or JSON Schema)"
2. Mapping of v0.5 field types to v1 FieldSpec kinds
3. Before/after examples
4. Codemod script (optional, if scope allows) at `packages/core/codemods/v0-to-v1.ts`

## 13. Phase 2 preview (v2 — AI launch)

(Designed in detail later in its own spec. Listed here for context.)

- `@rogeroliveira84/rdf-ai` package — `generateSchema({ prompt, from: 'text' | 'typescript' | 'zod' | 'json-sample' })`
- Backed by **Vercel AI SDK** with provider-agnostic model routing (Claude, GPT, Gemini via AI Gateway).
- Returns a Zod schema AND a JSON Schema (both).
- Playground at `docs.rdf.dev` with prompt box, live form preview, copy code button.
- Demo examples: "user signup", "invoice", "job application", "product config".
- **Conditional fields** (`dependsOn` metadata on FieldSpec) shipped alongside.
- **Multi-step wizard** (`<DynamicForm.Wizard>`) shipped alongside.
- **File upload** shipped alongside.

## 14. Out of scope (firmly)

- Rich text editor integration (tiptap) — heavy, can be user-provided as custom component
- Visual drag-and-drop form builder — separate project, maybe v3+
- React Native — focus is web; DOM coupling is fine
- Database/backend integration — renderer only, not full-stack
- Signature / geolocation / payment fields — niche, users can register custom fields

## 15. Timeline (rough)

| Phase | Weeks | Milestone |
|---|---|---|
| **P1.1 Monorepo migration** | 1 | pnpm+Turborepo set up, CRA removed, Vite+Vitest working, CI green |
| **P1.2 Core rewrite** | 1 | Zod/JSON Schema adapters, InternalSchema, RHF integration, all current field kinds rendering via shadcn |
| **P1.3 Docs & demo** | 1 | Fumadocs site, playground, migration guide, Storybook optional |
| **P1.4 Release** | 0.5 | Changesets, npm publish v1.0.0, README marketing, launch on r/reactjs |
| **P2.1 AI package** | 1.5 | Vercel AI SDK integration, prompt→schema, playground UI |
| **P2.2 Advanced fields** | 1 | Conditional, wizard, file, combobox |
| **P2.3 Marketing push** | 0.5 | Blog post, Twitter thread, Product Hunt, LinkedIn, dev.to |

Total: ~6–7 focused weeks. Calendar-wise with other obligations, plan for ~3 months to v2 ship.

## 16. Risks & mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| shadcn registry model still maturing in 2026 | Fallback: ship plain components as npm exports; registry is additive |
| `v0` / Cursor already do "prompt → form" | Differentiator is **quality of output** + **Zod/JSON Schema export** + **run-time renderer**, not "has AI" alone |
| Zod v4 breaking changes mid-project | Pin Zod version in peer deps, track migration actively |
| Legacy v0.5 users break on upgrade | Keep legacy adapter for 1 major, publish codemod, detailed migration guide |
| Solo maintainer burnout | Scope disciplined (§14 out-of-scope list); accept contributions early; use Changesets from day 1 so releases are low-friction |

## 17. Open questions (to decide during implementation)

- Tailwind 3 or 4? (Depends on shadcn registry compat at build time.)
- Docs site: Fumadocs vs Nextra vs Mintlify? (Lean Fumadocs for DX + OSS.)
- Which AI Gateway? (Lean Vercel AI Gateway for provider routing + cost tracking.)
- Bundle: single `core` package or split `core` / `adapters/zod` / `adapters/json-schema`? (Start single, split if bundle size pressure materializes.)

After user reviews this spec, next step is to invoke the `superpowers:writing-plans` skill to produce a detailed step-by-step implementation plan for **Phase 1** (the plan for Phase 2 gets written after Phase 1 ships).
